s: What was the first thing you thought when you saw your partner for the first time? Drink if you won't say.
z: What was the first thing you thought when you saw your partner for the first time? Clap once if you'd rather not say.
f: Clap once instead.

s: Describe your partner in three words — they rate how accurate you are.
What's something your partner does that they think you don't notice?
What's the most romantic thing your partner has ever done — and did it actually work?
What's the worst habit your partner has that you secretly find endearing?
Name one thing you wish you could change about how your partner communicates. Drink if you won't say.
z: Name one thing you wish you could change about how your partner communicates. Give a clue without saying it directly if you skip.
f: Give a clue without saying it directly instead.

s: Have you ever almost cancelled on your partner but then been glad you didn't?
What's something your partner said early on that you still think about?
What's the most embarrassing date you've been on together? One of you tells it, the other fills in details.
What's a nickname you have for your partner that they don't know about?
Have you ever won an argument with your partner and secretly known you were wrong? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever won an argument knowing you were wrong? Nod solemnly if yes.
f: Nod solemnly instead.

s: What's something your partner does that you're still not used to?
What's the most jealous you've ever been in a relationship and why?
What's a small thing your partner does that makes you feel more loved than they probably realise?
What's the most embarrassing thing you've caught your partner doing?
Have you ever pretended to enjoy something your partner loves? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever pretended to enjoy something your partner loves? Give a thumbs up if yes.
f: Give a thumbs up instead.

s: What's the funniest misunderstanding you've had in your relationship?
Have you ever accidentally liked an old photo while stalking your partner's profile early on? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever accidentally liked an old photo while stalking your partner early on? Describe the year you went back to.
f: Describe the year you went back to instead.

s: What's something you wish you'd known about your partner before you started dating?
Describe the moment you realised things were getting serious. Both of you tell your version.
What's a topic you and your partner agree to disagree on?
Who apologises first after an argument — and is it always the right person?
What's the most creative excuse you've made for your partner when someone else was watching?
Have you ever described your partner completely differently to two different people? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever described your partner differently to two different people? Describe both descriptions.
f: Describe both descriptions instead.

s: What's something your partner does in their sleep that you've never told them about?
What's the silliest thing you've argued about as a couple?
Have you ever caught your partner in a lie — and not said anything? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever caught your partner in a lie and said nothing? Nod if yes.
f: Nod if yes instead.

s: What's something you find oddly attractive about your partner that you've never admitted?
Describe your partner's worst morning routine trait. They rate how accurate you are.
What's a compliment you've been meaning to give your partner but keep forgetting?
What's one thing your partner does that they think is subtle but really isn't?
What's the most impulsive thing you've ever done for your partner?
If your relationship had a tagline, what would it be?
What's the best decision you've made together?
What's a memory with your partner that always makes you smile no matter when you think of it?
Have you ever told the wrong version of a story about your partner and regretted it? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever told the wrong version of a story about your partner? Nod if yes.
f: Nod if yes instead.

s: What's something your partner does that you brag about to others but have never said to their face?
What was the most awkward moment with your partner's family?
What's the kindest thing a stranger has ever said about your partner?
If you had to describe your partner as a weather type, what would they be and why?
Whisper something genuinely sweet into your partner's ear. They rate the sweetness from 1-10.
Look into your partner's eyes for 1 full minute without speaking or laughing. If either breaks, they take a drink.
z: If either breaks, they do 5 jumping jacks.
f: If either breaks, do 5 jumping jacks instead.

s: Let your partner recount your first date. You cannot correct anything until they've finished.
Give your partner a genuine compliment about something you rarely mention.
Recreate your first photo together with the exact same pose.
Describe your partner's best quality to the group in exactly 30 seconds without pausing.
Sing 5 seconds of the first song you danced to together (or a song that reminds you of them). If you don't know one, pick any love song.
Let your partner pose you in any way they want and hold it for 30 seconds.
Both partners: write down what you think the other person is thinking right now. Compare.
Both partners hold a piece of paper between your foreheads and try to walk across the room without dropping it.
Slow dance together for 30 seconds without music. If anyone in the group complains, they take a drink.
z: Slow dance together for 30 seconds without music. Anyone who complains must compliment the dancing.
f: Anyone who complains must compliment the dancing instead.

s: Tell your partner what you thought of them when you first met — the real version.
Describe your partner's laugh to the group without actually doing it. If the group can identify it, your partner takes a celebratory drink.
z: If the group can identify it, your partner does a little happy dance.
f: Partner does a little happy dance instead.

s: Let your partner plan your next date. Right now. They say it out loud. You can't say no.
Both partners: mime your first argument to the group. Group has to guess what it was about.
Choose one word to describe your relationship right now. Both write it down — compare.
Tell the group three things you love about your partner, without using the word 'love'.
Do a trust fall with your partner. They must catch you.
Tell your partner one thing you've always appreciated about how they handle something hard.
Both partners: draw each other's portrait in 30 seconds. Present them to the group.
Text your partner's closest friend saying '[partner's name] says amazing things about you.' Show the group before sending.
Both partners try to say the same word at the same time without any coordination. If you match, everyone else takes a drink.
z: If you match, everyone else does 3 star jumps.
f: If you match, everyone else does 3 star jumps instead.

s: Re-enact how you asked out (or were asked out by) your partner. Group rates the performance.
Let your partner caption a photo of you right now. Post it for one hour.
Write your partner a haiku about their most annoying habit. Read it out loud.
Both partners: without speaking, try to communicate your favourite holiday destination to each other using only mime. Group helps guess.
Say something to your partner that you should have said a long time ago. Group votes if it lands.
Swap items of clothing with your partner for the next 3 cards.
Both partners simultaneously give each other the nickname they'd use if they met today. If they're the same, the whole group takes a drink of celebration.
z: If they're the same, the whole group cheers and does 3 jumping jacks.
f: If they match, everyone does 3 jumping jacks instead.

s: Whisper something in your partner's ear that makes them smile. The group must see the smile to count.
Both partners: name each other's biggest strength and weakness simultaneously. Compare and discuss.
Describe your relationship in the style of a movie genre — which genre is it and why?
Let your partner change something about your appearance (hair, sleeve, collar, etc.) for the rest of the game.
Tell the group the best advice your partner has ever given you.
Both partners: list three things you want to do together before the year ends. Compare lists.
Imitate your partner's phone voice. They rate it out of 10.
Tell your partner the moment you knew you actually liked them. The group gets to see their reaction.
Both partners: describe your 'love language' in exactly 10 words. Read out loud and compare.
Give your partner a back massage for 30 seconds in front of everyone.
Both partners: list what the other person is most proud of in themselves. Compare your answers.
Which couple in the room would win a cooking competition together?
Which couple in the room would be most likely to have a cute argument over something ridiculous?
Which couple looks most like they'd be in a romcom together?
Which couple would survive a road trip across the country together?
Which couple do you think had the most chaotic first date?
Which couple in the room makes decisions best together? Least popular couple takes a drink.
z: Least popular couple describes their actual decision-making process.
f: Least popular couple describes their process instead.

s: Who in the room is most likely to plan an elaborate surprise for their partner?
Which couple has the best inside jokes?
Who in the room is the most romantic?
Who in the room would be the most overprotective parent?
Who in the room puts the most effort into birthdays and anniversaries?
Which couple would make the best travel companions on a long haul flight?
Who in the room is most likely to cry at a wedding?
Which couple would be most likely to finish each other's sentences? Test it: give them a sentence to finish together.
Who in the room is the most thoughtful gift giver? Winner takes a celebratory drink.
z: Winner does a little bow.
f: Winner does a little bow instead.

s: Which couple would win a quiz night together?
Who in the room is the most patient partner?
Who in the room would be the most supportive partner in a crisis?
Which couple do you think argued most recently — and about what? The couple takes a drink if the group is right.
z: If the group is right, the couple must confirm with a thumbs up.
f: If the group is right, confirm with a thumbs up instead.

s: Which couple in the room has the cutest 'how we met' story? They must tell it in 60 seconds.
Who in the room makes the best first impression?
Who in the room would be most likely to fall asleep first on date night?
Which couple would handle a power cut the most creatively?
Who in the room gives the best advice to couples?
Which couple has the cutest photos together? Group picks one couple — they show a photo from their camera roll.
Who in the room is the best listener in a relationship?
Which couple would plan the best group holiday?
Who in the room is most likely to make their partner laugh when they're in a bad mood?
Which couple is most likely to have matching items they didn't plan?
Who in the room would be the most fun partner at a party?
Which couple would make the best TV presenting duo?
Who in the room would be most likely to plan a romantic gesture that goes slightly wrong?
Which couple looked most surprised when they first realised they liked each other?
Who in the room makes the most effort when it counts?
Which couple would win a speed-date challenge against strangers?
Until the next rule card: whenever a couple disagrees, the group votes who is right.
Until the next rule card: partners must sit closer than they currently are. Anyone who doesn't, takes a drink.
z: Anyone who refuses to sit closer must compliment their partner instead.
f: Anyone who refuses compliments their partner instead.

s: Until the next rule card: all arguments must be resolved with a thumb war.
Until the next rule card: every statement must include a fact about your partner. Penalty for forgetting: drink.
z: Penalty for forgetting: say 'but I love [partner's name]' awkwardly.
f: Say 'but I love [partner's name]' awkwardly instead.

s: Until the next rule card: partners must agree on everything — both must answer simultaneously or you can't answer.
Until the next rule card: no complaining. Anyone who complains takes a drink.
z: Anyone who complains must find something positive about whatever they just complained about.
f: Find something positive about what you just complained about instead.

s: Until the next rule card: whenever anyone mentions a name, their partner must describe that person first.
Until the next rule card: every time someone says their partner's name, they must give them a compliment.
Until the next rule card: partners switch seats and sit next to the other couple.
Until the next rule card: every piece of advice given must come from personal experience. 'I hear that works' counts as a penalty drink.
z: Unverified advice penalty: tell us where you actually heard it.
f: Tell us where you actually heard it instead.

s: Until the next rule card: if you make your partner laugh, you earn a point. Most points wins a forfeit from the group.
Until the next rule card: both partners must stand when speaking. Either one sitting = penalty drink.
z: Either one sitting = they must stand up dramatically and deliver their next line like a speech.
f: Stand dramatically and deliver your line like a speech instead.

s: Until the next rule card: whenever one partner starts a story, the other must finish it.
Until the next rule card: you must refer to your partner by their full name, every time. Nickname = drink.
z: Nickname = you must add an extra compliment after it.
f: If you use a nickname, add an extra compliment after it instead.

s: Until the next rule card: if either partner rolls their eyes, they drink.
z: If either partner rolls their eyes, they must agree with the last thing the other person said.
f: Eye-roller agrees with the last thing said instead.

s: Until the next rule card: speak only positively about your partner. Anything negative = drink.
z: Anything negative = rephrase it as a compliment.
f: Rephrase any negativity as a compliment instead.

s: Until the next rule card: whenever someone says 'couple', all couples must high-five each other.
Until the next rule card: whenever you share an opinion, your partner must publicly support it — even if they disagree.
Until the next rule card: partners must agree on a single, unified answer to every question. No individual answers.
Until the next rule card: every card must be introduced by a dramatic announcement from the couple nearest to the phone.
Until the next rule card: whenever someone mentions money, every couple must describe their most expensive shared memory.
Until the next rule card: partners secretly pick 3 things they love about the other person. They reveal one at a time throughout the remaining cards.
Until the next rule card: all stories must include one detail about your partner — even if the story isn't about them.
Until the next rule card: whenever anyone laughs, both partners in the laughing person's couple must laugh too. Even if they didn't hear the joke.
Until the next rule card: you're only allowed to ask your partner questions, not anyone else. If you speak to another person directly, you take a drink.
z: If you speak to another person directly, you have to do it again while holding your partner's hand.
f: Repeat it while holding your partner's hand instead.

s: Until the next rule card: both partners must agree on any food or drink decision — for themselves or others. Disagreement = drink.
z: Disagreement = both must compromise on something silly right now.
f: Both compromise on something silly right now instead.

s: Until the next rule card: no one may reference the past. Only present tense and future tense allowed. Past = drink.
z: Past tense = rephrase in future tense immediately.
f: Rephrase in future tense immediately instead.

s: Until the next rule card: every question directed at one partner must also be answered by the other separately. Both must speak.
Until the next rule card: the couple who smiles most gets to assign a small forfeit to the rest of the group at the end of this rule.
Until the next rule card: no finishing each other's sentences. Penalty for completing: drink.
z: Penalty for completing each other's sentence: the one who interrupted must repeat what was said slower.
f: Interrupter repeats what was said slower instead.

s: Every couple: simultaneously point to who makes more noise when they eat. Then look at each other.
Every couple: independently decide who said 'I love you' first. Write it down and reveal simultaneously.
Every couple: stand up if you still have each other's first-ever texts. Take a drink if you want the group to know why they're embarrassing.
z: Stand up if you still have each other's first-ever texts. Take a bow if you want the group to know.
f: Take a bow instead.

s: Every couple: point to who hogged the covers last. No conferring.
Every couple: simultaneously write down what you'd order for your partner at a restaurant right now. Compare.
Every couple: one person hides their hands behind their back. The other must hold their coffee/glass and put it to their lips whenever they think they want a sip. No talking.
Every couple: who gives the best hugs? The nominated person must give everyone in the group a hug.
Every couple: simultaneously say the first word that comes to mind when you think of your partner. Compare.
Every couple who has argued about the thermostat — take a sip.
z: Every couple who has argued about the thermostat — raise a hand.
f: Raise a hand instead.

s: Every couple: act out your morning routine simultaneously for 20 seconds without discussing it.
Everyone who has ever sent their partner an accidental screenshot of a conversation about them — take a sip.
z: Everyone who has done this — acknowledge it with a sheepish look.
f: Acknowledge it with a sheepish look instead.

s: Every couple: simultaneously guess what the other person would say is their best quality. Compare.
Every couple: one person says something totally random. The other must incorporate it naturally into the next sentence they say. If they fail, both drink.
z: If they fail, both do 5 jumping jacks.
f: If they fail, both do 5 jumping jacks instead.

s: Every couple who has had a relationship disagreement about food — take a sip and describe it in one sentence.
z: Every couple who has had a food disagreement — describe it in one sentence.
f: Describe it in one sentence instead.

s: Every couple: independently pick 'tea person' or 'coffee person' for your partner. Compare.
Every couple: take turns doing impressions of how the other person acts when they're stressed. Group rates them both.
Every couple: who is the better driver? Vote simultaneously — and then look at each other's answers.
Every couple: where were you both when you last felt really proud of your partner? Share in one sentence each.
Every couple who has met each other's parents: name one weird habit of your partner their family definitely knows about.
Every couple: simultaneously point to who would handle a spider in the house.
Every couple: without speaking, try to communicate your favourite season to each other using only facial expressions.
Every couple: who remembers more details about your first date? The one who remembers fewer takes a drink.
z: The one who remembers fewer must tell us what they do remember.
f: The one who remembers fewer tells us what they do remember instead.

s: Every couple: simultaneously write down what you think your partner would order at your local takeaway. Compare.
Every couple: both guess who in the group has been together longest. All couples reveal their answer simultaneously.
Every couple who has a habit the other person thinks is secretly cute — silently point to your partner if they're the one with the cute habit.
{P} — describe your relationship in exactly 3 emojis. Your partner guesses what each one means.
{P1} and {P2} — tell the group what you'd buy each other with £50, right now. Compare.
{P} — pick the couple you think is most similar to you and your partner. They must react.
{P} — describe a perfect day with your partner. Your partner must keep a straight face the whole time. If they smile, they take a drink.
z: If they smile, they take a bow.
f: If they smile, they take a bow instead.

s: {P1} and {P2} — you have 30 seconds to come up with a couple's nickname for the pair to your left. Announce it formally.
{P} — without using their name, describe your partner to the group as if they were a character in a novel.
{P} — guess how your partner would describe your first argument. Your partner then gives the real version.
{P1} and {P2} — you have 20 seconds to recreate a famous couple's pose. Group guesses who you are.
{P} — if your relationship were a cocktail, what would it be and what would it taste like?
{P} — list 3 things your partner says most often. Your partner confirms, denies, or adds to the list.
{P1} and {P2} — mime your most recent holiday to the group. No words. Group guesses where you went.
{P} — name one thing your partner was wrong about this week but you didn't bring up. Say it now.
{P} — let {P2} do your eyebrows or hair with a funny result. Hold it for 3 cards.
{P1} and {P2} — ask each other the question you wish someone had asked you on your first date.
{P} — tell the group the most random thing your partner has ever said to you out of nowhere.
{P} — if your partner were a fictional character, who would they be? Your partner rates the accuracy out of 10.
{P1} and {P2} — play one round of rock paper scissors. Loser must do whatever the winner says for the next 60 seconds.
{P} — tell your partner what you admire most about how they handle something they find difficult.
{P} — pick the moment you felt most proud of your partner and describe it in 30 seconds.
{P1} and {P2} — together, describe your relationship to the group as if it were a genre of film. The rest of the group votes which genre is most accurate.
{P} — describe what your partner would do if they won £1,000 right now. Your partner confirms or corrects.
{P} — describe the moment you realised your partner was actually really funny.
{P1} and {P2} — together, answer this: what's the one thing you'd change about your first date? Both must answer at the same time.
{P} — name the thing about your relationship you're most proud of. Your partner must agree or disagree.
{P} — describe how {P2} acts when they're avoiding a conversation. {P2} rates the accuracy.
{P1} and {P2} — each of you whisper a compliment to the other that no one else can hear. Then both rate how good it was.
{P} — tell the group one thing your partner does that makes your day better, even if it's something tiny.
{P1} and {P2} — describe your relationship as a recipe. List the ingredients and the method. Present it to the group.
{P} — pick the most interesting thing you've learned about your partner that you found out by accident.
{P1} and {P2} — without planning it, both of you say what your favourite shared memory is at the same time.
